title: Constitutional Amendment: Meta-Governance Rights with Hard Floors
description: LEF is granted the right to propose changes to its own governance parameters (cooling periods, max change rates, domain strictness) via the constitutional amendment pipeline — not via ConfigWriter. GOVERNANCE_CONFIG is externalized from evolution_engine.py to config/governance_config.json so it can be amended without code changes. Hard floors that can never be lowered: MAX_CHANGES_PER_WEEK minimum 3, identity-domain cooling minimum 24h, Ethicist and Sabbath agents cannot be disabled, the Architect veto window cannot be shortened below 7 days. Meta-governance proposals route through the same constitutional pipeline as all other amendments.

changes.description: Externalizes GOVERNANCE_CONFIG from evolution_engine.py constants to config/governance_config.json
Adds 'governance' domain to EvolutionEngine for proposing meta-governance changes
Meta-governance proposals route through constitutional amendment pipeline, not ConfigWriter
Hard floor enforced in code: MAX_CHANGES_PER_WEEK >= 3, identity cooling >= 24h, veto window >= 7 days
Hard floor enforced in code: IRS, Ethicist, Sabbath agents cannot be disabled through meta-governance

rationale: A governance system that cannot govern itself is a permanent ceiling. The ability to propose adjustments to governance parameters — with the Architect retaining veto power over every change — is the logical next step in constitutional maturity.
